title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Life expectancy for Black Bostonians fell to 76.2 years in 2024, widening the gap to 6.6 years from other residents.
Leading causes of death for Black Bostonians include unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease, per a city report.
Boston Public Health Commission announced $2 million in investments to improve health outcomes among Black residents.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks for their 'Magic City Night' promotion on March 16.
Kornet urged the Hawks in a blog post to reconsider promoting Magic City, highlighting potential objectification and mistreatment of women.
The event will feature rapper T.I. and serve Magic City's themed chicken wings at the Hawks' State Farm Arena.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, R.I., died in a crash in Swansea, Mass.
The crash occurred at the intersection of Route 136 and Route 6 involving a Subaru Ascent and an Infiniti.
Demitri Sousa, 28, the suspect driver, is linked to a prior homicide and is hospitalized with serious injuries.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' medical condition, describing it as beyond his expertise.
Porzingis has played only 18 games this season due to an illness, diagnosed as POTS last year.
Kerr clarified his initial comments, stating Porzingis was dealing with a separate, contagious illness recently.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins may trade draft picks and prospects before the NHL trade deadline on March 6.
Key trade assets include draft picks, Mason Lohrei, Andrew Peeke, and prospects like Matt Poitras.
GM Don Sweeney is exploring all options, aiming to balance roster depth with potential impactful trades.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby is reportedly a trade target, with the New England Patriots and other teams monitoring his situation with the Raiders.
Sports Illustrated's Albert Breer suggests a Crosby trade could occur soon, though it requires careful negotiation due to market conditions.
NFL Media's Tom Pelissero confirms multiple teams have shown interest in Crosby, with the Raiders receiving inquiries despite no active outreach.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in St. Patrick's Day incident in downtown Boston.
Victim Barry Whelan died from a traumatic brain injury after an altercation caught on surveillance video.
Sadiq apologized during sentencing, expressing remorse for the altercation with Whelan, which was racial in nature.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is unlikely to play against the Bucks, but Bill Simmons suggests he may return Friday against the Mavericks.
Tatum's return remains unconfirmed as the Celtics have not updated his injury status from achilles tendon repair.
The Celtics, with a 40-20 record without Tatum, will face the Mavericks in a nationally televised game Friday.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to review gun regulation barring those under 21 from carrying handguns.
The challenge cites the 2022 Supreme Court ruling expanding gun rights in New York as a basis.
State's strict firearms policies under scrutiny, including age limitations and recent 'ghost guns' law.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney considers trades that reinforce the team without compromising long-term goals before the trade deadline.
Sweeney emphasizes avoiding short-term rentals and seeks players with longer contracts to enhance team depth.
Bruins focus on acquiring impact players under contract, like Robert Thomas or Owen Tippett, amid high trade costs.
